demo1/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the Spring Boot parent version to 3.4.4 to align with the Java 21 migration.
demo1/pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.x requires Java 17+ and uses the Jakarta EE namespace (jakarta.* instead of javax.*); keeping version 2.6.3 would cause classpath conflicts and missing class errors when running on Java 21 with Spring Boot 3 dependencies.

demo1/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property to 21 to target the new JDK.
demo1/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the build would target Java 11, potentially missing out on Java 21 features or failing if the project structure enforces strict JDK alignment, though primarily this is a configuration update to match the runtime environment.

demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.general_idea: Remove deprecated WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter and migrate to component-based SecurityFilterChain configuration.
demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.why_exists: WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter is deprecated in Spring Security 5.7 and removed in Spring Security 6 (required by Spring Boot 3.x); extending it causes compilation errors or runtime failures in the new environment.

demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.general_idea: Replace configure(AuthenticationManagerBuilder) with a dedicated AuthenticationProvider bean.
demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.why_exists: The configure(AuthenticationManagerBuilder) method is part of the deprecated WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter. In Spring Security 6, authentication providers must be defined as beans.

demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.general_idea: Update AuthenticationManager bean definition to use AuthenticationConfiguration.
demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.why_exists: super.authenticationManagerBean() is no longer available because the class no longer extends WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter. The new way to expose the AuthenticationManager is via AuthenticationConfiguration.

demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.general_idea: Replace configure(HttpSecurity) with a SecurityFilterChain bean using lambda DSL.
demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.why_exists: The configure(HttpSecurity) method is part of the deprecated WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter. Spring Security 6 requires defining the security chain as a SecurityFilterChain bean. Additionally, methods like authorizeRequests() and antMatchers() are removed/renamed in favor of authorizeHttpRequests() and requestMatchers().

demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.general_idea: Update imports to reflect new Spring Security 6 API classes.
demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.why_exists: Old imports (WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter, AuthenticationManagerBuilder) are removed or deprecated. New imports (SecurityFilterChain, AuthenticationProvider, etc.) are required for the new configuration style.
